# UC-22.10.39 · VIP / High-Profile Patient — Elevated Access Monitoring

## Description

Applies extra scrutiny to any user accessing patients on the VIP/high-profile flag list to deter and detect snooping.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic and sourcetypes epic:audit, epic:audit`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) VIP list restricted to small privacy group managing lookup; (2) real-time alert to privacy on-call; (3) never place plaintext celebrity names in Splunk—use surrogate IDs only. Indexes required: `index=epic`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `sourcetype=epic:audit``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup vip_patients.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `vip_level`, `start_date`, `end_date`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) VIP list restricted to small privacy group managing lookup; (2) real-time alert to privacy on-call; (3) never place plaintext celebrity names in Splunk—use surrogate IDs only.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup vip_patients.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT vip_level, start_date, end_date
| where isnotnull(vip_level) AND _time>=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d") AND _time<=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup vip_care_team.csv PAT_ID USER_ID OUTPUT is_authorized
| where isnull(is_authorized)
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, vip_level, Activity
```

Understanding this SPL

**VIP / High-Profile Patient — Elevated Access Monitoring (§164.502(a) / policy)** — Applies extra scrutiny to any user accessing patients on the VIP/high-profile flag list to deter and detect snooping.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup vip_patients.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `vip_level`, `start_date`, `end_date`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic; **sourcetype**: epic:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=epic, sourcetype="epic:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(vip_level) AND _time>=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d") AND _time<=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(is_authorized)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, Activity, PAT_ID, USER_ID, vip_level.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (masked IDs only), Single value (unauthorized VIP touches).

## SPL

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup vip_patients.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT vip_level, start_date, end_date
| where isnotnull(vip_level) AND _time>=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d") AND _time<=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup vip_care_team.csv PAT_ID USER_ID OUTPUT is_authorized
| where isnull(is_authorized)
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, vip_level, Activity
```

## Visualization

Table (masked IDs only), Single value (unauthorized VIP touches). Key fields: `Activity`, `PAT_ID`, `USER_ID`, `vip_level`.
